results.title: # Complete DEI Audit Summary - Housing Authority of Clackamas County
results.snippet: **Detailed Policy Analysis** - Compare Oregon findings against Housing Authority policies - Identify conflicts with Bondi memo guidance - Draft remediation recommendations --- ## Success Metrics | Objective | Target | Status | |-----------|--------|--------| | Discover Oregon DEI policies | 15+ | ✅ 24 documents | | Identify housing-specific policies | 10+ | ✅ 22 documents | | Find OHCS governance documents | 2+ | ✅ 4 board packets | | Rate documents for risk | 100% | ✅ 24/24 scored | | Create se

matched_rules.text: **Detailed Policy Analysis** - Compare Oregon findings against Housing Authority policies - Identify conflicts with Bondi memo guidance - Draft remediation recommendations --- ## Success Metrics | Objective | Target | Status | |-----------|--------|--------| | Discover Oregon DEI policies | 15+ | ✅ 24 documents | | Identify housing-specific policies | 10+ | ✅ 22 documents | | Find OHCS governance documents | 2+ | ✅ 4 board packets | | Rate documents for risk | 100% | ✅ 24/24 scored | | Create searchable index | 1 | ✅ JSON + CSV | | Generate priority analysis | 1 | ✅ Multiple docs | | Identify Clackamas County links | TBD | ⏳ Manual search needed | --- ## Technical Details ### Search Queries Used **Run 1 (Broad):** ``` site:oregon.gov filetype:pdf "diversity" OR "equity" OR "DEI" site:oregon.gov filetype:pdf "underrepresented" OR "underserved" site:oregon.gov filetype:pdf "cultural competence" OR "lived experience" site:clackamas.us filetype:pdf "diversity" OR "equity" site:oregonlegislature.gov filetype:pdf "diversity" OR "equity" ``` **Run 2 (Housing-Focused):** ``` site:oregon.gov filetype:pdf "housing" AND ("diversity" OR "equity" OR "DEI") site:oregon.gov filetype:pdf "affordable housing" AND ("equity" OR "diversity") site:oregon.gov/biz/dsc filetype:pdf "diversity" OR "equity" site:oregon.gov/lcd filetype:pdf "housing" AND ("diversity" OR "equity") site:oregon.gov filetype:pdf "fair housing" AND ("equity" OR "diversity") site:clackamas.us filetype:pdf "housing" AND ("diversity" OR "equity") site:clackamas.us/housing filetype:pdf "diversity" OR "equity" OR "DEI" site:clackamas.us filetype:pdf "fair housing" OR "housing authority" site:clackamas.us filetype:pdf "procurement" AND ("diversity" OR "minority" OR "women") site:clackamas.us filetype:pdf "diversity" AND ("policy" OR "plan" OR "action plan") ``` ### Keyword Matching Algorithm **DEI Keywords (16):** diversity, equity, DEI, inclusion, underserved, underrepresented, DEIB, equitable **Proxy Keywords (9):** BIPOC, cultural competence, lived experience, equity plan, safe space, environmental justice, underserved communities **Binding Indicators (11):** shall, must, required, mandatory, agreement, contract, policy, statewide, applicable, ordinance, standard **Scoring Logic:** - Score 3: All three keyword types present - Score 2: Two keyword types present (usually DEI + binding) - Score 1: One keyword type present - Score 0: No keywords detected --- ## Conclusion The DEI Compliance Audit for Housing Authority of Clackamas County has completed its **collection phase** with comprehensive discovery of 24 Oregon state and local documents containing DEI language and binding policy indicators.

**Operational Mandates** - Specific requirements for housing programs ### What We Found 4 OHCS Board packets (Jan, Nov, Dec 2025) all rated **SCORE 3** with: - Explicit DEI language - Binding indicators: "shall", "must", "statewide", "policy", "contract", "required", "mandatory" - Direct applicability to housing authorities ### Action Item **CRITICAL:** Review these board packets to identify: - Any explicit DEI mandates for housing authorities - Funding conditions related to DEI - Compliance requirements - Mentions of Clackamas County Housing Authority --- ## Risk Assessment Per Bondi Memo Categories ### Category 1: Preferential Treatment **Documents Found:** 18+ documents with "diversity", "underrepresented", "BIPOC" language **Risk:** HIGH - Language suggests diversity-based preferences in hiring and selection **Example:** "equity plan", "BIPOC", "lived experience", "underrepresented" ### Category 2: Proxy Criteria **Documents Found:** 16+ documents with proxy language **Risk:** HIGH - "Underserved", "disadvantaged", "underrepresented" could serve as proxies for race **Example:** DEI plans tie housing/hiring to geographic equity (zip codes), education level, language ### Category 3: Segregation **Documents Found:** 12+ documents with segregated program language **Risk:** MEDIUM - Some documents reference separate tracks or targeted programs **Example:** "Targeted recruitment", "community-specific outreach", "populations experiencing homelessness" ### Category 4: Selection Practices **Documents Found:** 21+ documents with hiring/selection mandates **Risk:** HIGH - DEI action plans explicitly require diversity in hiring committees, selection criteria **Example:** "Diverse hiring panel", "diversity requirement", "equity-focused recruitment" ### Category 5: Training Mandates **Documents Found:** 21+ documents with training requirements **Risk:** HIGH - DEI/DEIB training appears mandatory in many state plans **Example:** "Mandatory DEI training", "cultural competence requirements", "implicit bias training" ### Category 6: Third-Party Funding **Documents Found:** 18+ documents reference grants/federal funding **Risk:** HIGH - May create binding compliance obligations **Example:** "Federal grant condition", "funding requirement", "HUD grant compliance" --- ## Clackamas County Status ### Current Search Results The Brave API rate-limited before completing searches of site:clackamas.us.

- **Queries Attempted:** 4 Clackamas-specific searches - **Results:** All returned 429 (Rate Limited) errors - **Status:** Manual follow-up needed ### Recommended Next Step **Manual Web Search:** Go to Clackamas County government website and search for: - Housing Authority policies - DEI/diversity policies - Hiring practices - Procurement policies - Board meeting minutes (2024-2025) **Resources:** - Clackamas County website: clackamas.us - Clackamas County Housing Authority: clackamas.us/housing or housing.clackamas.us - County Board minutes, policies, plans --- ## Data Summary ### Collection Statistics | Metric | Value | |--------|-------| | Total PDFs Downloaded | 17 (8 new in run 2) | | PDFs Successfully Parsed | 15 (2 corrupted/invalid) | | Documents Indexed | 24 (consolidated after dedup) | | Total Raw Data | 42MB | | Total Parsed Text | ~1.2MB | | Analysis Timestamp | 2025-12-31T00:12:52 UTC | ### Risk Distribution ``` Score 3 (Critical): ██████████████ 12 documents (50%) Score 2 (High): ███████████ 11 documents (46%) Score 1 (Monitor): █ 1 document (4%) ``` ### Applicability Distribution ``` Housing Policies: ████████████████████████ 22 (92%) Hiring Policies: ███████████████████████ 21 (88%) Training Requirements: ███████████████████████ 21 (88%) Procurement DEI: ██████████████████ 18 (75%) ``` --- ## Files & Locations ### Summary Reports - [FINDINGS_20251231_001230.txt](research_results/FINDINGS_20251231_001230.txt) - Latest one-page summary - [FINDINGS_20251231_000908.txt](research_results/FINDINGS_20251231_000908.txt) - First run summary - [HOUSING_FOCUS_FINDINGS.md](HOUSING_FOCUS_FINDINGS.md) - Housing-specific analysis ### Detailed Reports - [DETAILED_REPORT_20251231_001230.txt](research_results/DETAILED_REPORT_20251231_001230.txt) - Latest technical report - [DETAILED_REPORT_20251231_000908.txt](research_results/DETAILED_REPORT_20251231_000908.txt) - First run report ### Data Files - [document_index_20251231_001230.json](research_results/document_index_20251231_001230.json) - Full searchable index - [summary_20251231_001230.csv](research_results/summary_20251231_001230.csv) - Spreadsheet with all documents ### Raw Documents - PDFs: `research_results/documents/raw/` (26 files, 42MB) - Parsed Text: `research_results/documents/parsed/` (24 files + metadata JSON) ### Framework Documents - [agent.md](agent.md) - Bondi memo + audit framework + search strategy - [EVIDENCE_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md](EVIDENCE_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md) - Email template for evidence collection - [FINDINGS_PRIORITY_ANALYSIS.md](FINDINGS_PRIORITY_ANALYSIS.md) - Tier 1/2/3 priority guide - [QUICK_START.md](QUICK_START.md) - How to use the toolkit --- ## Next Actions (Prioritized) ### IMMEDIATE (Today/Tomorrow) 1.

results.title: What Is Resident Retention (and How Can It Be Improved)? - Quantum Residential
results.snippet: 4.28.22 Putting a plan in place to manage resident retention and keep tenant satisfaction high is one of the most important parts of being a property manager.

matched_rules.text: 4.28.22 Putting a plan in place to manage resident retention and keep tenant satisfaction high is one of the most important parts of being a property manager.
Hiring a property management team that specializes in increasing tenant retention in multifamily communities can help boost your profits while keeping tenant satisfaction high.

Hosting community events or setting aside opportunities for your tenants to make the most of community amenities—like offering complimentary coffee mornings, throwing informal tenant get-togethers around holidays, or facilitating the forming of a casual basketball team—can go far in building community and making tenants want to stay.

results.title: Crystal Temple, Author at Quantum Residential
results.snippet: Hiring a property management team that specializes in increasing tenant retention in multifamily communities can help boost your profits while keeping tenant satisfaction high.

matched_rules.text: Hiring a property management team that specializes in increasing tenant retention in multifamily communities can help boost your profits while keeping tenant satisfaction high.

Hosting community events or setting aside opportunities for your tenants to make the most of community amenities—like offering complimentary coffee mornings, throwing informal tenant get-togethers around holidays, or facilitating the forming of a casual basketball team—can go far in building community and making tenants want to stay.

How Hosting Community Events Can Help By planning out your retention strategies and setting aside a budget for events focused on building community, you can make tenants feel welcomed into their new home from the first day they arrive.

results.snippet: - Areas: hiring/promotions, procurement/contracts/subgrants, program eligibility and participation, training programs, facilities policies (restrooms/locker rooms), third-party agreements, complaint/retaliation procedures.

matched_rules.text: - Areas: hiring/promotions, procurement/contracts/subgrants, program eligibility and participation, training programs, facilities policies (restrooms/locker rooms), third-party agreements, complaint/retaliation procedures.
Interview stakeholders and collect complaints/retaliation reports.

# Agent Audit Guide — Attorney General Bondi Memo (structured) Purpose - Provide a concise, actionable audit guide for an automated or human agent to evaluate policies and programs of the Housing Authority of Clackamas County, Clackamas County, and the State of Oregon for practices contrary to the Attorney General guidance (Bondi memo, July 29, 2025).

matched_entities.name: Interview stakeholders and collect complaints/retaliation reports.
- Evidence: policy documents, scholarship criteria, selection committee minutes.
- Review hiring/admissions policies for "preferential" language or required demographic minimums ("diverse slate", quota
